methods.description: Process for obtaining radiocarbon dates from core samples using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS), preparing different sample types (plant macrofossils, charcoal, bulk sediment), and calibrating the raw dates to calibrated years Before Present (BP).

steps.description: A total of 12 accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) 14 C dates were measured at the Poznań Radiocarbon Laboratory and at the Radiocarbon Dating Center in Lyon. Plant macrofossils (plant fibers, wood) and charcoal were selected for four samples, and bulk sediment was used for eight samples according to the sediment type.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform AMS 14C dating on selected core sample materials
inputs: Selected material types (plant macrofossils, charcoal, bulk sediment)

steps.description: Radiocarbon ages were calibrated in calibrated years BP using the Calib 8.2 software with the IntCal20 calibration curve (Reimer et al., 2020).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate raw radiocarbon ages to calendar years BP using specified software and calibration curve

methods.description: Process for identifying, extracting, preparing, and geochemically analyzing volcanic glass shards (tephra) from core samples to use as chronological markers by correlating them with known eruptions based on their geochemical composition.

steps.description: Visible tephra layers and cryptotephra layers, detected by magnetic susceptibility and XRF core scanning data, were subsampled and processed for geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify tephra layers (visible and cryptotephra) using magnetic susceptibility and XRF core scanning data

steps.description: Visible tephra layers and cryptotephra layers... were subsampled and processed for geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Subsample core material at identified tephra layer depths

steps.description: Cryptotephra were extracted using H 2 O 2 and HCl to remove organic matter and carbonates
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically process cryptotephra subsamples to remove organic matter and carbonates
Sieve processed tephra material at specified mesh sizes

steps.description: ...and volcanic glass shards were embedded in resin...
steps.searchable_summary: Embed volcanic glass shards in resin for analysis

steps.description: ...sectioned and polished for electron probe microanalysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Section and polish embedded tephra samples for microanalysis

steps.description: A JEOL-JXA8230 probe at the Helmholtz Centre Potsdam (Germany) was used with a 15 kV accelerating voltage, 10 nA beam current and a 15 µm beam size. Analytical count times were 20 s for all elements except for K and Na, measured first at 10 s.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform electron probe microanalysis (EPMA) on prepared tephra samples
inputs: Analytical settings (15 kV, 10 nA, 15 µm beam size, count times)

steps.description: International glass standards, such as the Max Planck Institute (MPI-glasses) ATHO-G, StHs6/80 and GOR-132 (Jochum et al., 2006) and the natural Lipari obsidian (Hunt and Hill, 1996;Kuehn et al., 2011), were measured prior to sample analysis for data quality assurance.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze international glass standards using EPMA for data quality control

steps.description: Geochemical glass data of the Matese tephra are normalized on an anhydrous, volatile-free basis
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize raw geochemical data on an anhydrous, volatile-free basis

steps.description: ...and compared with published tephra glass datasets (Wulf et al., 2008;Smith et al., 2011;Tomlinson et al., 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare normalized tephra geochemical data with published datasets to identify known eruptions and correlate ages
inputs: Published tephra glass datasets (Wulf et al., Smith et al., Tomlinson et al.)

methods.description: Method for building a chronological model relating depth in the core to age, primarily based on selected reliable radiocarbon dates and correlated tephra ages, using an interpolated linear curve with specified software.

steps.description: The overall age-depth model of the Matese core is based on imported, well-accepted tephra ages and one 14 C date of a bulk sediment sample from the bottom of the core at 534 cm (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Select reliable chronological control points from calibrated radiocarbon and correlated tephra ages

steps.description: The age-depth model based on one radiocarbon date and correlated tephra ages was constructed using an interpolated linear curve with the R Clam program using 95 % confidence intervals (Blaauw, 2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Build an age-depth model using linear interpolation with R Clam software
outputs: Age-depth model (function or table mapping depth to age)
95% confidence intervals for age at each depth

methods.method_name: Age-Depth Model Validation using Pollen Stratigraphy
methods.description: Method for validating the constructed age-depth model by comparing the ages of key vegetation transitions identified in the core's pollen stratigraphy with established ages for these transitions from regional pollen records.

steps.description: In order to validate the age-depth models, the pollen stratigraphy of the regional sites was compared with pollen data at Matese.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare pollen stratigraphy of the Matese core with regional pollen stratigraphy sites
inputs: Regional pollen stratigraphy data (Pavullo, Accesa, Albano, Mezzano, Monticchio, Trifoglietti)
outputs: Identified similarities/differences in pollen patterns between Matese and regional sites

steps.description: The regional pollen stratigraphy is used to validate this age-depth model... The pollen stratigraphy of Pavullo nel Frignano (Vescovi et al., 2010) and lakes Accesa (Drescher-Schneider et al., 2007), Albano (Mercuri et al., 2002), Mezzano (Sadori, 2018), Monticchio (Allen et al., 2002) and Trifoglietti (De Beaulieu et al., 2017) were used to identify the OD-B/A, B/A-YD and YD-Holocene transitions.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify depths corresponding to key vegetation transitions (OD-B/A, B/A-YD, YD-Holocene) in Matese pollen data based on regional patterns
inputs: Knowledge of regional pollen patterns for key transitions
outputs: Depths of key vegetation transitions in Matese core

steps.description: We used the median age for each transition... The ages obtained with the regional pollen stratigraphy show an OD-B/A transition at 14 500 ± 93.7 cal BP, a B/A-YD transition at 12 800 ± 57.7 cal BP and a YD-Holocene transition at 11 575 ± 103.1 cal BP (Allen et al., 2002;Mercuri et al., 2002;Drescher-Schneider et al., 2007;Vescovi , 2010;De Beaulieu et al., 2017;Sadori, 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain established median ages and uncertainties for key regional vegetation transitions from published studies
inputs: Published studies on regional pollen stratigraphy and chronology
outputs: Median ages and uncertainties for OD-B/A, B/A-YD, YD-Holocene transitions

steps.description: Pollen stratigraphy of the regional sites was compared with pollen data at Matese, and the ages obtained show a good correspondence with the ages of tephra samples but a poor correspondence with the 14 C dates. Therefore, most of the 14 C dates (Table 3) are not included in the age-depth model (except the date at the base of the core). The ages of tephra samples and ages constrained from the pollen stratigraphy are in good agreement, contrasting results from the 14 C dates which are randomly scattered and systematically too young (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Compare age-depth model ages at identified transition depths with established regional transition ages to validate the model
inputs: Depths of key vegetation transitions in Matese core
Established regional median ages for transitions
outputs: Assessment of agreement/disagreement between model ages and validation ages
Validation status of the age-depth model
